title: cerberiopsis candelabra
intro: Cerberiopsis candelabra, also known as the candelabra tree, is a plant species native to New Caledonia, belonging to the family Monimiaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bark of Cerberiopsis candelabra contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and infections.
